# Fake Transport Runtime Vectors

These vectors characterize deterministic fake-transport controls before connection-independent runtime orchestration moves into shared. `FakeTransportContractCommonTest.kt` consumes the base service-readiness policy as executable shared commonTest coverage, while Android and iOS facade tests remain responsible for public error mapping and feature-specific readiness behavior before production delegation.

`scripted-command-outcomes-policy.json` pins command ordering, write payload capture, and scripted success/error/complete outcomes. `delayed-response-policy.json` pins delayed-response polling, response release, and poll observation on a virtual clock. `reconnect-after-failure-policy.json` pins explicit reconnect-after-failure behavior and command recording for retry planning without platform callback ownership. `retry-delay-policy.json` pins retry delay scheduling, retry count observation, and elapsed retry times on a virtual clock. `unscripted-operation-timeout-policy.json` pins deterministic timeout behavior and command recording when no scripted outcome exists. `service-readiness-policy.json` pins delayed readiness, missing readiness timeout, attempt ordering, timeout labeling, and wall-clock-free execution. `virtual-clock-timeout-policy.json` pins virtual-time advancement and timeout checks without sleeping on platform protocol constants. `fake-transport-readiness.json` names the shared-contract behavior-family gate for these base fake-transport controls: scripted command ordering, write-payload capture, scripted success/error/complete outcomes, delayed-response polling, delayed-response release, reconnect-after-failure, reconnect command recording, retry-delay scheduling, retry count observation, unscripted-operation timeout, unscripted command recording, service-readiness delayed success, service-readiness timeout, readiness attempt ordering, virtual-clock timeout boundaries, wall-clock-free timeout tests, platform facade compatibility gates, and the `compile-verification-gate` must remain covered before production runtime orchestration delegates to shared code. Future fake-transport vectors should stay scoped to transport controls that shared runtime code can own without importing Android Bluedroid or iOS CoreBluetooth behavior.
